# Last Time

::: {.incremental}
+ **Variable Details — sizes, fixed-width integers, type casting**
:::

::: notes
Week 14 lecture 01 covered data type sizes, fixed-width integers (int32_t etc.), and explicit type conversion with static_cast.
:::

---

# Today's Agenda

- Reference Variables — Review
- What's the Output?
- Heap vs. Stack
- new and delete
- Dynamic Arrays

::: notes

:::

{{< include topic_1_reference_review.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_2_whats_the_output.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_3_heap_vs_stack.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_4_new_and_delete.qmd >}}
{{< include topic_5_dynamic_arrays.qmd >}}

---

# Key Takeaways

- A reference is an alias — same memory, no pointer syntax needed
- Stack memory is automatic and scope-bound; heap memory is manual and persistent
- `new` allocates heap memory; `delete` releases it — you own the cleanup
- Use `new[]` and `delete[]` for arrays; set pointers to `nullptr` after release

---

# Next Time

- What's the Output? (new/delete mechanics)
- Memory Leaks
- Dangling Pointers After delete
- Smart Pointers Callback
- Fill In the Missing Code (×2)
- Programming Task — Parallel Dynamic Arrays

---

# Appendix

---

## Reference Variables — Review: Good Practices

---

## Reference Variables — Review: Common Mistakes

---

## Reference Variables — Review: More Examples

---

## Reference Variables — Review: Practice Problems

---

## Reference Variables — Review: AI Search Terms

---

## Reference Variables — Review: Questions for Reflection

---

## Heap vs. Stack: Good Practices

---

## Heap vs. Stack: Common Mistakes

---

## Heap vs. Stack: More Examples

---

## Heap vs. Stack: Practice Problems

---

## Heap vs. Stack: AI Search Terms

---

## Heap vs. Stack: Questions for Reflection

---

## new and delete: Good Practices

---

## new and delete: Common Mistakes

---

## new and delete: More Examples

---

## new and delete: Practice Problems

---

## new and delete: AI Search Terms

---

## new and delete: Questions for Reflection

---

## Dynamic Arrays: Good Practices

---

## Dynamic Arrays: Common Mistakes

---

## Dynamic Arrays: More Examples

---

## Dynamic Arrays: Practice Problems

---

## Dynamic Arrays: AI Search Terms

---

## Dynamic Arrays: Questions for Reflection
